L1.area: Expression evaluation and property access resolution logic

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists due to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) mismatch in property key validation. The sandbox validates property access keys by coercing them to strings during safety checks, but does not enforce that the same string value is used during the actual property lookup. An attacker can supply a specially crafted object whose string coercion returns different values on successive calls, returning a benign key during the security check and a dangerous key during the actual access. This allows bypassing prototype access restrictions and achieving arbitrary code execution outside the sandbox.

L3.area: Expression evaluation and property access resolution logic
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists due to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) mismatch in property key validation. The sandbox validates property access keys by coercing them to strings during safety checks, but does not enforce that the same string value is used during the actual property lookup. An attacker can supply a specially crafted object whose string coercion returns different values on successive calls, returning a benign key during the security check and a dangerous key during the actual access. This allows bypassing prototype access restrictions and achieving arbitrary code execution outside the sandbox.

ground_truth.reason: A TOCTOU mismatch in property key validation allows attackers to pass objects with custom string coercion that return different values during the safety check versus the actual property access, enabling sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.
